rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type scope
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Use valid implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Align helper declaration types with the expression to avoid type mismatches
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence
Branching logic should use nested `if ... then ... else`
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid component type scopes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
